L1.area: Byte serialization and deserialization infrastructure for composite data types

L2.description: A vulnerability exists where uninitialized memory can be dropped during deserialization of fixed-size arrays. When individual elements are being deserialized one by one and any element's deserialization panics, the destructor is invoked on memory that was never properly initialized, leading to undefined behavior including potential information disclosure from reading uninitialized bytes.

L3.area: Fixed-size array deserialization within the byte struct macro system
L3.description: A vulnerability exists in the deserialization of fixed-size arrays from raw bytes where unsafe uninitialized memory allocation is used. When constructing an array element by element, if any element's deserialization panics, the array's drop handler executes on elements that were never initialized, resulting in undefined behavior and potential information disclosure from uninitialized memory.

ground_truth.reason: The read_bytes_default_le and read_bytes_default_be functions use std::mem::uninitialized() to allocate an array, then fill it element by element; if an element's deserialization panics, the Drop implementation runs on uninitialized memory, causing undefined behavior.
